brief.para: Store-release a single-element structure from one lane of one register

authored.para: This instruction stores the specified element of a SIMD&FP register to memory.
The instruction also has memory ordering semantics, as described in Load-Acquire, Load-AcquirePC, and Store-Release. For information about addressing modes, see Load/Store addressing modes.
Depending on the settings in the CPACR_EL1, CPTR_EL2, and CPTR_EL3 registers, and the current Security state and Exception level, an attempt to execute the instruction might be trapped.

operationalnote_content.para: This instruction is a data-independent-time instruction as described in About PSTATE.DIT.

a.hover: Is the name of the first or only SIMD&FP register to be transferred, encoded in the "Rt" field.
For the "64-bit", "64-bit, immediate offset", and "64-bit, register offset" variants: is the element index, encoded in "Q".
Is the 64-bit name of the general-purpose base register or stack pointer, encoded in the "Rn" field.

ps.name: A64.ldst.asisdlso.STL1_asisdlso_D1

intro.para: Is the name of the first or only SIMD&FP register to be transferred, encoded in the "Rt" field.
Is the element index, encoded in "Q".
Is the 64-bit name of the general-purpose base register or stack pointer, encoded in the "Rn" field.

ps.name: A64.ldst.asisdlso.STL1_asisdlso_D1
A64.ldst.asisdlso.STL1_asisdlso_D1
